title: Discovery Service: Sentinel Lifecycle Manager (Heartbeat, Decommission, Rejoin/Migration)
description: Implement the complete sentinel lifecycle management: heartbeat registration to maintain liveness state, automatic status transitions (active → degraded → offline) based on heartbeat freshness, admin-initiated decommission with credential revocation and descriptor invalidation, and re-join flow for migrated or recovered sentinels. All lifecycle transitions are audit-logged.
objectives: Discovery always has an accurate liveness picture via heartbeat TTL-based status transitions
Decommission is atomic and cascades to credential revocation and descriptor invalidation
Re-join after migration produces a new registration without leaking identity from old deployment

steps: Step 1 - Heartbeat Endpoint: POST /api/v1/sentinels/heartbeat. Auth: sentinel bearer token. Request body: { sentinel_id, instance_id, version (sentinel software version str), health: { cpu_pct: float, mem_pct: float, request_rate: float (optional) } }. Validates that sentinel_id matches token subject. Updates: sentinel_instances row for instance_id (last_seen=now(), status='active', metadata.health=health). Updates sentinels.last_seen=now(). Heartbeat interval: configured in config bundle (default 30s). Returns 200 { acknowledged_at, next_expected_at }.

Step 2 - Sentinel Status State Machine: Three statuses tracked in sentinels.computed_status (NOT persisted, computed by background task). active: last_seen < now() - HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT (default 90s). degraded: last_seen between 90s and 300s. offline: last_seen > 300s or is_active=false. Background task (every 60s): compute status for all sentinels, write computed_status to DB, emit Prometheus metric sentinel_status_gauge (labels: env, role).

Step 3 - Instance Registry: sentinel_instances table (TASK-022). Each instance heartbeat upserts its row. Multiple instances of the same sentinel (same sentinel_id) represent multi-instance deployment. Instance status transitions: active → draining (set by admin before graceful shutdown) → offline. Discovery reports per-sentinel aggregate status (e.g., active if ANY instance is active).

Step 4 - Decommission Endpoint: POST /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/decommission. Auth: security-admin. Body: { reason, revoke_credentials: bool (default true), invalidate_descriptor: bool (default true) }. Steps: (a) Set sentinels.is_active=false and computed_status='offline'. (b) If revoke_credentials: call CredentialIssuerService (or StatusListService) to revoke all active credentials for subject_did=sentinel.did. (c) If invalidate_descriptor: call DescriptorService.invalidate(service_id, env) — mark descriptor inactive, remove from resolve results. (d) Set all sentinel_instances for this sentinel to status='offline'. (e) Generate a terminal config bundle (all_requests_denied=true) per TASK-027. (f) Write audit event: action='sentinel_decommissioned', actor, sentinel_id, reason.

Step 5 - Decommission Confirmation Gate: For prod sentinels: decommission requires a two-step confirmation (like dual-control in TASK-030). POST /decommission creates a pending decommission record. Security-admin must POST /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/decommission/confirm within 15 minutes. Cancellable via DELETE /decommission/pending.

Step 6 - Rejoin Endpoint: POST /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/rejoin. Auth: mTLS + sentinel bearer token OR new enrollment token (if old token expired). Body: { did, proof (DID PoP), migration_token (optional — from TASK-049 sentinelctl migrate), new_instance_id, new_base_url }. Validates: new DID matches old sentinel record (same identity, possibly same key or rotated key with PoP). If DID key rotated: requires migration_token signed with both old and new key (TASK-039). On success: update sentinels.did (if rotated), set is_active=true, update last_seen, generate new config bundle. No new enrollment token needed for same-identity rejoin.

Step 7 - Heartbeat Staleness Alerting: If a sentinel transitions from active to degraded: emit Prometheus alert sentinel_degraded{sentinel_id, env, role}. On degraded → offline: emit sentinel_offline alert. These feed into the Grafana dashboard and PagerDuty integration (TASK-063). Write system audit event on each transition.

Step 8 - Admin Force-Offline: PATCH /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/status { status: 'offline' }. Auth: operator. Does NOT revoke credentials — just marks the sentinel as offline for service discovery. Used during planned maintenance. Reversal: status='active' with fresh heartbeat.

Step 9 - Sentinel Listing with Status: GET /api/v1/sentinels. Auth: viewer. Query params: env, role, status (active|degraded|offline), service_id. Cursor-paginated. Response includes computed_status, last_seen, instance_count, active_instance_count.

Step 10 - Lifecycle History: sentinel_lifecycle_events table (append-only): event_type (heartbeat|status_change|decommission|rejoin), sentinel_id, old_status, new_status, instance_id, ts, actor_id. Used for audit export (TASK-034). Keep 90 days.

files.services/discovery/src/discovery/tasks/h: Background task: status transitions based on TTL

error_handling.condition: Heartbeat sentinel_id does not match token
error_handling.message: Sentinel ID in request does not match authenticated sentinel
Decommission confirmation window expired. Re-initiate decommission.

error_handling.condition: Rejoin with mismatched DID and no migration token
error_handling.message: DID does not match original sentinel identity. Provide migration_token for DID rotation.

security_constraints: Heartbeat endpoint validates sentinel_id matches bearer token subject — no impersonation
Decommission cascades to credential revocation — a decommissioned sentinel cannot use old credentials
Rejoin with a different DID requires migration_token signed with both old and new keys
All lifecycle transitions write to audit_events and sentinel_lifecycle_events

acceptance_criteria: Heartbeat updates last_seen and instance status within 1 second
Status monitor transitions sentinel to 'degraded' after 90s without heartbeat (integration test with time mock)
Decommission revokes all credentials and returns 404 on resolve for that service's descriptor
Rejoin with same DID succeeds and generates a new config bundle
